children.heading: Adequate and timely reports to Secretary of State; maintenance of records
children.label: a Adequate and timely reports to Secretary of State; maintenance of records

children.text: sales of defense articles or defense services under , or of design and construction services under ;section 2762 of this titlesection 2769 of this title
commercial sales of defense articles or defense services licensed or approved under ; orsection 2778 of this title
exports of defense articles or defense services pursuant to a treaty referenced in ;section 2778(j)(1)(C)(i) of this title

children.heading: Presidential regulation
children.label: b Presidential regulation
children.text: The President may, by regulation, prohibit, limit, or prescribe conditions with respect to such contributions, gifts, commissions, and fees as he determines will be in furtherance of the purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: Allocation to contract; improper influence
children.label: c Allocation to contract; improper influence
children.text: No such contribution, gift, commission, or fee may be included, in whole or in part, in the amount paid under any procurement contract entered into under section 2762 or , unless the amount thereof is reasonable, allocable to such contract, and not made to a person who has solicited, promoted, or otherwise secured such sale, or has held himself out as being able to do so, through improper influence. For the purposes of this section, “improper influence” means influence, direct or indirect, which induces or attempts to induce consideration or action by any employee or officer of a purchasing foreign government or international organization with respect to such purchase on any basis other than such consideration of merit as are involved in comparable United States procurements.section 2769 of this title

children.heading: Availability of records to Congress and Federal agencies
children.label: d Availability of records to Congress and Federal agencies

children.text: All information reported to the Secretary of State and all records maintained by any person pursuant to regulations prescribed under this section shall be available, upon request, to any standing committee of the Congress or any subcommittee thereof and to any agency of the United States Government authorized by law to have access to the books and records of the person required to submit reports or to maintain records under this section.

Access by an agency of the United States Government to records maintained under this section shall be on the same terms and conditions which govern the access by such agency to the books and records of the person concerned.
